title: Integration tests + CI (BATS + GitHub Actions + goreleaser)
description:
End-to-end BATS test suite, GitHub Actions CI workflow, and goreleaser distribution config covering all instill commands.

## What to build

A complete quality gate and distribution pipeline:

1. **BATS test suite** — functional tests for each command: check-skills (happy path, orphan removal, broken removal, create missing), init-project (normal, --force, --skills, git warning), show-library (in/out of project, --filter), pick-skills (add, remove, all-or-nothing validation), add-hooks (add, idempotent, no-TTY no-op). Test error exits (1, 2, 3).
2. **GitHub Actions** — on push/PR: go build, go vet, golangci-lint, BATS suite against a fixture library. Build matrix for darwin/linux/amd64/arm64.
3. **goreleaser** — .goreleaser.yaml for multi-platform binary releases. Makefile with build, test, lint, install targets.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] BATS tests cover happy path for all five commands
- [ ] BATS tests cover all documented error exit codes (1, 2, 3)
- [ ] CI runs on push and PR to main
- [ ] CI builds and vets for darwin and linux, amd64 and arm64
- [ ] goreleaser produces binaries for darwin-amd64, darwin-arm64, linux-amd64, linux-arm64
- [ ] make build, make test, make install all work
- [ ] All tests pass on a clean checkout with only SKILL_LIBRARY_PATH set

## Blocked by

instill-mat, instill-d6p, instill-hs0, instill-qu4, instill-dyt, instill-rp6
notes: Carry-forward from pick-skills TUI task: Bubbletea is now a direct dependency and go mod tidy is clean. CLI has injectable pickSkillsTUI and isTTY seams used by tests. init-project no --skills now preflights TTY before writing project files, including --force, and --force only authorizes overwrite before launching TUI. ApplySkillSelection centralizes full selection writes/diff/reconcile; BATS integration tests should cover arg mode, TUI non-TTY error/no-write behavior, init-project --skills, init-project no-skills non-TTY, add-hooks no-TTY, and stale manifest cleanup if practical.

title: add-hooks command
description:
Implements instill add-hooks to inject the instill check-skills SessionStart hook into .claude/settings.json.

## What to build

instill add-hooks merges a SessionStart hook entry into .claude/settings.json so that instill check-skills runs automatically at the start of each Claude Code session.

- No TTY: exit 0 silently (hooks are dev-time, not CI concern).
- Requires manifest; exits 1 if absent.
- Read .claude/settings.json (create empty {} if absent).
- Check if a SessionStart hook with command 'instill check-skills' already exists.
- If present: print 'already configured' and exit 0.
- If absent: merge new hook entry idempotently into the hooks.SessionStart array, write atomically.
- Print 'added SessionStart hook: instill check-skills' on success.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Exits 0 silently when no TTY available
- [ ] Exits 1 with correct message if no manifest found
- [ ] Creates .claude/settings.json if absent
- [ ] Adds SessionStart hook with command 'instill check-skills' and type 'command' under matcher ''
- [ ] Idempotent: prints 'already configured' and exits 0 if hook already present
- [ ] Does not duplicate hook entries on repeated runs
- [ ] Writes settings.json atomically
- [ ] Preserves existing settings.json content

## Blocked by

instill-d6p
notes:
Review closure for add-hooks:

/golang-cli requested preserving nested hook data, command-only idempotency, manifest parsing in TTY mode, and clarity on the no-TTY exception. Implemented raw JSON tree mutation for settings, changed idempotency to detect existing command regardless of type, parsed the manifest before settings edits when stdin is a TTY, and kept no-TTY as an intentional command-specific no-op before manifest/config work per this bead.

/golang-code-style approved with no requested changes. The missing-manifest wording note was addressed by matching SPEC.md exactly: error: no manifest found — run 'instill init-project' first.

/golang-design-patterns requested raw JSON preservation and merging into an existing empty SessionStart matcher instead of appending a duplicate matcher. Implemented both to preserve the Claude settings ownership boundary and avoid matcher order ambiguity.

/golang-linter approved with no requested changes after go test, go vet, and golangci-lint.

/golang-safety requested preserving nested hooks, preserving existing settings.json mode, and exact missing-manifest wording. Implemented raw JSON preservation, mode preservation with 0644 only for newly-created settings, and exact message alignment.

/golang-testing requested exact hook-shape assertions, TTY missing-manifest coverage, nested hook preservation coverage, and merge-preservation cases. Added tests for exact matcher/hook shape, simulated TTY no-manifest behavior, malformed manifest in TTY mode, preservation of nested hook fields/events, mode preservation, and command-only idempotency.

Conflict resolution:
- No-TTY add-hooks skips manifest/config work even though SPEC.md has global command pre-run rules. Chosen path: preserve the bead-specific no-TTY no-op so automation and noninteractive shells stay quiet; enforce manifest validation for TTY runs before mutating settings.
- Atomic writes use the shared unique-temp atomic writer rather than a literal fixed .tmp path. Chosen path: keep unique temp files because they are safer under concurrent processes while preserving atomic same-directory replacement semantics.

Verification after resolved findings:
- go test ./... passed (62 tests)
- go test -race ./... passed
- go vet ./... passed
- golangci-lint v2 run passed with 0 issues
- go build passed
close_reason: Implemented add-hooks with TTY gating, manifest validation, raw JSON settings preservation, idempotency, mode preservation, and reviewer-requested tests; all Go gates pass.

title: pick-skills TUI (interactive skill selector)
description:
Implements the Bubbletea TUI for interactive skill selection, used by pick-skills (no args) and init-project (no --skills flag).

## What to build

An interactive terminal UI using Bubbletea that allows the user to browse and toggle skills:

- Pre-populate the list from the library; check skills already in manifest.
- Space bar to toggle selection.
- / key to activate fuzzy search filter.
- Confirm (Enter) computes diff (added, removed), writes manifest atomically, runs reconciler.
- Escape/q cancels without changes.
- Used from two entrypoints: pick-skills with no args, and init-project with no --skills flag (replacing the placeholder 'use pick-skills' message from earlier slices).

Print diff output on confirm: 'added: <name>', 'removed: <name>', 'manifest: N skills'.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] TUI launches from 'instill pick-skills' (no args) when manifest exists
- [ ] TUI launches from 'instill init-project' (no --skills flag) after project files are created
- [ ] Skills pre-checked according to current manifest
- [ ] Space toggles a skill on/off
- [ ] / activates fuzzy search; typing filters the list
- [ ] Enter confirms, writes manifest atomically, runs reconciler, prints diff output
- [ ] Escape/q cancels without modifying manifest
- [ ] Works correctly with large libraries (200+ skills)

## Blocked by

instill-d6p, instill-qu4
notes:
Review closure for pick-skills TUI:

/golang-cli requested that init-project --force without --skills launch the TUI instead of silently resetting to empty. Implemented force as overwrite permission only; all no---skills init flows now call the skill picker after project files are created. Added CLI coverage for --force launching the picker.

/golang-code-style requested go mod tidy because Bubbletea was marked indirect and go.sum lacked tidy checksums. Ran go mod tidy; go mod tidy -diff is now clean.

/golang-design-patterns requested handling stale manifest skills that are no longer in the library and the same --force TUI correction. Implemented library-backed initial TUI selection so invisible stale entries are not retained, and ApplySkillSelection removes stale manifest entries on confirm with diff output. Added regression tests for both stale selection and stale removal.

/golang-linter approved with no requested changes after gofmt, go test, go vet, and golangci-lint.

/golang-safety requested preflighting TTY availability before init-project writes files when it would launch the TUI. Implemented CLI TTY preflight for no---skills init flows while preserving the existing manifest already exists error path. Added a regression test proving non-TTY init exits 2 and leaves no .claude or .gitignore behind.

/golang-testing requested real RunPickSkillsTUI boundary coverage, q cancel coverage, toggle-off coverage, and reconciling diff-output spacing. Added non-TTY RunPickSkillsTUI no-change coverage, q cancel coverage, prechecked toggle-off coverage, and kept padded diff output intentionally.

Conflict resolution:
- Diff output spacing: the bead prose says added: <name>, while SPEC.md examples and existing arg mode use padded added:   <name>. Chosen path: keep padded output for consistency with SPEC.md and existing command output; documented this in the task notes.
- Stale manifest cleanup before TUI: reviewers suggested either pre-reconcile before launch or filter initial selection and remove stale entries on confirm. Chosen path: filter initial selection and remove on confirm so the TUI stays quiet until the user confirms, then prints the explicit removed: line.
- init-project --force empty reset: current behavior was convenient, but SPEC.md says only --skills skips the TUI. Chosen path: force only authorizes overwrite; no---skills still launches TUI.

Verification after resolved findings:
- go test ./... passed (74 tests)
- go test -race ./... passed
- go vet ./... passed
- golangci-lint v2 run passed with 0 issues
- go build passed
- go mod tidy -diff clean
close_reason: Implemented Bubbletea pick-skills TUI, no-arg command launch, init-project launch including --force, non-TTY safety, stale manifest cleanup, and review-requested tests; all Go gates pass.

title: show-library command
description:
Implements instill show-library with optional project-state annotations and --filter flag.

## What to build

instill show-library reads the library directory, lists all skills (subdirs containing SKILL.md), sorted alphabetically, and prints them.

- Inside a project (manifest present): prefix each skill with [✓] if selected, [ ] if not.
- Outside a project: plain list, no annotations.
- --filter <substring>: case-insensitive substring match on skill name, applied after sorting.
- Footer line: 'N skills  (M selected)' inside project, 'N skills' outside.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Lists all library subdirectories that contain SKILL.md, sorted alphabetically
- [ ] Annotates with [✓]/[ ] when run inside a project with a manifest
- [ ] Prints plain list with no annotations when run outside a project
- [ ] --filter performs case-insensitive substring match on skill name
- [ ] Footer shows skill count and selected count (inside project) or skill count only (outside)
- [ ] Works correctly when library is empty

## Blocked by

instill-mat
notes:
Review lanes completed for instill-hs0:
- /golang-cli: requested implicit reconciliation before listing when a manifest is present.
- /golang-code-style: requested selected footer count describe visible filtered results.
- /golang-design-patterns: requested SkillExists return filesystem errors instead of collapsing them to missing, and selected count from displayed rows.
- /golang-linter: approved; golangci/vet/test gates passed.
- /golang-safety: requested selected count from filtered visible rows; path/nil/write behavior otherwise acceptable.
- /golang-testing: requested invalid library-entry tests and exact output assertions for annotated/filtered output.

Conflict resolution:
- show-library side effects: implemented implicit reconciliation for in-project runs despite read-style command expectations. Rationale: SPEC.md defines reconciliation as global behavior when a manifest is present. To reduce noise, implicit reconciliation reports only removed skills and suppresses created/ok lines.
- Selected footer count: changed to count only selected skills visible in the filtered rendered rows. Rationale: the footer reads as a summary of the displayed result set; total project selection count would be ambiguous after filtering.
- SkillExists API: changed from bool to (bool, error). Rationale: callers need to distinguish unknown skills from unreadable library state.

Executed actions:
- Added show-library command with --filter.
- Added annotated and plain library rendering.
- Added implicit reconciliation for in-project show-library, with removal-only reporting.
- Added exact unit/CLI output tests, invalid library entry coverage, filtered selected-count coverage, and missing-library reconciliation coverage.

Validation:
- go test ./...
- go test -race ./...
- go vet ./...
- golangci-lint v2 run ./...
- go build -o instill .
close_reason: Implemented show-library with six-lane review findings resolved and validation passing.

title: pick-skills command (arg mode + --remove)
description:
Implements instill pick-skills with positional skill-name args and --remove flag for manifest management.

## What to build

instill pick-skills <name...> adds named skills to the project manifest. instill pick-skills --remove <name,...> removes them.

- Requires manifest; exits 1 if absent.
- Validation is all-or-nothing: check ALL provided names against library before changing anything. If any unknown, exit 1 with correct message; make no changes.
- Merge valid names into manifest (deduplicated, sorted).
- --remove: remove named skills from manifest (names not present are silently ignored).
- Write manifest atomically: write to .claude/skill-manifest.json.tmp then rename.
- Run reconciler after any change.
- Print 'added: <name>' and/or 'removed: <name>' for each change; 'manifest: N skills' at end.
- With no args and no --remove: launch TUI (deferred to TUI slice — exit 1 with 'no skills specified' for now).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Exits 1 with correct error if no manifest found
- [ ] Validates ALL args before making any changes; rejects entire set if any unknown
- [ ] Adds named skills to manifest (deduplicated, sorted alphabetically)
- [ ] --remove removes named skills from manifest
- [ ] Manifest write is atomic (tmp file → rename)
- [ ] Reconciler runs after change
- [ ] Correct output lines: 'added:', 'removed:', 'manifest: N skills'
- [ ] Invoking with no args and no --remove exits 1 (TUI not yet implemented)

## Blocked by

instill-mat
notes:
Review lanes completed for instill-qu4:
- /golang-cli: requested --remove library validation and noted fixed temp-name vs unique temp-name tradeoff.
- /golang-code-style: requested --remove validation and raised added: padding inconsistency.
- /golang-design-patterns: requested validation of union of add/remove names before manifest reads/writes.
- /golang-linter: approved; test/vet/golangci passed.
- /golang-safety: requested --remove validation and raised added: padding inconsistency; safety otherwise acceptable.
- /golang-testing: requested unknown remove and mixed add/remove regression coverage plus no-manifest stderr assertion.

Conflict resolution:
- --remove validation: implemented validation of the normalized union of add/remove names against the library before reading or writing the manifest. Rationale: SPEC.md and bead acceptance both say all provided names must be validated all-or-nothing.
- added: padding: kept the aligned 'added:   <name>' output. Rationale: SPEC.md examples use the aligned form, and this preserves visual alignment with 'removed:'.
- Manifest temp file name: kept unique temp files instead of literal skill-manifest.json.tmp. Rationale: prior safety review found fixed temp paths race under concurrent runs; unique temp + rename better satisfies atomicity and reproducibility even though pseudocode names a simple tmp path.

Executed actions:
- Added pick-skills command with positional add args and --remove CSV.
- Added all-or-nothing library validation for add and remove names.
- Added manifest add/remove/dedupe/sort behavior and reconciliation.
- Added package and CLI tests for add, remove, unknown add, unknown remove, mixed add/remove failure, no manifest, and no args.

Validation:
- go test ./...
- go test -race ./...
- go vet ./...
- golangci-lint v2 run ./...
- go build -o instill .
close_reason: Implemented pick-skills arg mode and --remove with six-lane review findings resolved and validation passing.

title: init-project command (non-TUI path)
description:
Implements instill init-project with --skills and --force flags, creating the manifest, symlink directory, and .gitignore entry.

## What to build

instill init-project sets up a project for instill management. It must:

1. Check for existing .claude/skill-manifest.json — exit 1 with message if found and --force not set.
2. Warn if no .git directory is present (continues, does not fail).
3. Create .claude/ directory if absent.
4. Write .claude/skill-manifest.json with {"skills": []}.
5. Create .claude/skills/ directory if absent.
6. Inject into .gitignore (create if absent): a comment line and .claude/skills/ — only if not already present.
7. If --skills provided: validate all names against library (all-or-nothing — reject entire set if any unknown), write manifest, reconcile symlinks, exit.
8. If no --skills: launch pick-skills TUI (deferred to TUI slice — for now, just print 'use pick-skills to add skills' and exit 0).

Print structured output lines: 'initialized:', 'created:', 'updated:', 'warning:' as shown in the spec.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Creates .claude/skill-manifest.json with empty skills array when none exist
- [ ] Exits 1 with correct message if manifest already exists and --force not set
- [ ] --force overwrites existing manifest
- [ ] Creates .claude/skills/ directory
- [ ] Creates .gitignore if absent; appends .claude/skills/ entry with comment if not present
- [ ] --skills flag validates all names all-or-nothing; rejects with correct error if any unknown
- [ ] --skills flag writes manifest and reconciles symlinks on success
- [ ] Prints warning if not a git repo; continues without error
- [ ] Prints structured output lines matching spec

## Blocked by

instill-mat
notes:
Review lanes completed for instill-d6p:
- /golang-cli: requested exact structured output and .gitignore text, plus pre-write --skills validation for all-or-nothing behavior.
- /golang-code-style: requested wrapping long error/write calls.
- /golang-design-patterns: requested avoiding durable side effects on invalid --skills, exact warning output, and CLI boundary tests.
- /golang-linter: requested v2 golangci config fix, checked output writes, gosec/revive handling, and unused parameter cleanup.
- /golang-safety: requested .gitignore symlink protection, force-empty reconciliation to clear stale symlinks, and CRLF/anchored .gitignore idempotency.
- /golang-testing: requested exact output assertions, CLI tests for init-project flags/errors, --skills atomicity decision, and .gitignore idempotency coverage.

Conflict resolution:
- --skills all-or-nothing vs SPEC step order: chose pre-write validation and full command atomicity for --skills. Rationale: the bead acceptance says --skills validation is all-or-nothing, and this prevents data loss under --force --skills docker,missing. The SPEC step order is implementation guidance, but the durable safety contract is stronger.
- .gitignore symlink behavior: reject symlinked .gitignore with exit 3 instead of following it. Rationale: following user symlinks can write outside the project root; refusing is deterministic and safer.
- Revive package/export comments: configured revive to ignore package-comments/exported comments for this small internal CLI while keeping errcheck/gosec/staticcheck/govet active. Rationale: avoids low-signal comment churn while preserving behavioral lint coverage.
- Gosec test warnings: excluded gosec for _test.go fixtures. Rationale: temp-dir fixture permissions and variable test paths are not product risk; production gosec findings remain active with targeted justifications.

Executed actions:
- Added init-project command with --skills and --force.
- Added InitProject implementation for manifest, .claude/skills, .gitignore, warning, and reconciliation.
- Validated --skills before project writes and preserved existing manifests on invalid forced init.
- Matched spec warning/comment text and output ordering.
- Reconciled stale symlinks on --force empty reinit.
- Added .gitignore symlink rejection and CRLF/anchored idempotency handling.
- Added CLI and package tests for success, --force, existing manifest, invalid skills, warning output, idempotent .gitignore, and symlinked .gitignore.

Validation:
- go test ./...
- go test -race ./...
- go vet ./...
- golangci-lint v2 run ./...
- go build -o instill .
close_reason: Implemented init-project non-TUI path with six-lane review findings resolved and validation passing.

title: CLI scaffold + check-skills reconciler
description:
Foundational Go module with Cobra CLI skeleton, config resolution, and the core symlink reconciler that all other commands depend on.

## What to build

Bootstrap the Go module (cobra, go.mod), wire up the top-level CLI entry point, and implement two things end-to-end:

1. **Config resolution** — SKILL_LIBRARY_PATH env var → ~/.config/instill/config.json → TTY interactive prompt (writes config) → exit 2 with message when no TTY and no config.
2. **instill check-skills** — reads .claude/skill-manifest.json, reconciles .claude/skills/ symlinks in three passes: (a) remove orphaned symlinks not in manifest, (b) remove broken symlinks whose library target is gone and drop name from manifest, (c) create missing absolute symlinks for names in manifest. Prints removed/created lines; prints 'ok: N skills linked' at the end; silent when nothing changed. No-op exit 0 when no manifest found.

Exit codes: 0 success, 1 malformed manifest, 2 environment/config error, 3 filesystem error.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] go build ./... produces a binary named instill
- [ ] SKILL_LIBRARY_PATH env var is used when set; otherwise reads ~/.config/instill/config.json; prompts on TTY if neither present; exits 2 without TTY
- [ ] instill check-skills removes symlinks present in .claude/skills/ but absent from manifest
- [ ] instill check-skills removes broken symlinks (library target gone), removes name from manifest, prints 'removed: <name> (no longer in library)'
- [ ] instill check-skills creates missing absolute symlinks (.claude/skills/<name> -> <library_path>/<name>)
- [ ] Prints 'ok: N skills linked' on completion; silent when no changes needed
- [ ] Exits 0 silently when run outside a project (no manifest)
- [ ] Exits 1 on malformed manifest JSON
- [ ] Exits 3 on filesystem error creating or removing symlinks

## Blocked by

None — can start immediately
notes:
Review lanes completed for instill-mat:
- /golang-cli: requested spec-correct ~/.config/instill/config.json, real TTY detection, stderr prompt discipline, and explicit decision on orphan removal output.
- /golang-code-style: requested documented exported APIs, go mod tidy, flatter guard clauses, and less duplicated CLI cwd setup.
- /golang-design-patterns: requested real TTY detection, safe manifest skill-name validation, malformed manifest precedence over config resolution, and go mod tidy.
- /golang-linter: requested manifest path validation, .golangci.yml, go mod tidy, and orphan-removal output decision.
- /golang-safety: requested manifest path traversal prevention, unique temp-file atomic writes, and atomic config writes.
- /golang-testing: requested exit-code 3 coverage, CLI-level check-skills coverage, prompt/config tests, and stronger reconciler assertions.

Conflict resolution:
- Orphan symlink removal output: kept orphan removals silent except for the final ok line. Rationale: SPEC.md explicitly requires the detailed removed line for skills no longer in the library, while orphan cleanup is an internal reconciliation operation. This keeps command output focused on manifest-affecting changes and avoids surprising users with names they did not select.
- Config resolution global vs check-skills no-project behavior: kept check-skills as a no-manifest silent exception before config resolution. Rationale: SPEC.md says out-of-project check-skills exits 0 silently, which would be broken by unconditional root-level config resolution.
- PTY vs injection for testing prompts: used direct prompt helper tests plus golang.org/x/term for real runtime detection. Rationale: deterministic tests avoid OS-sensitive PTY behavior while runtime detection is still production-grade.

Executed actions:
- Added strict single-path-element skill validation for manifest/library joins.
- Added unique temp-file atomic writes for manifest/config.
- Switched config path to ~/.config/instill/config.json.
- Switched TTY detection to golang.org/x/term and prompts/config-written diagnostics to stderr.
- Ensured malformed manifests are parsed before config resolution in check-skills.
- Added .golangci.yml and ran go mod tidy.
- Added CLI/reconciler/config tests for valid linking, malformed manifest, no config exit 2, filesystem exit 3, invalid skill names, config path, and prompt helper behavior.

Validation:
- go test ./...
- go test -race ./...
- go vet ./...
- go build -o instill .
close_reason: Implemented CLI scaffold, config resolution, and check-skills reconciler with six-lane review findings resolved and validation passing.
